Br₂Sc₂Te₂ semiconductor

📐 P1 (#1) 🧩 basis: Br_px, Br_py, Br_pz, Br_s, Sc_dx2-y2, Sc_dxy, Sc_dyz, Sc_dz2, Sc_dzx, Sc_px, Sc_py, Sc_pz, Sc_s, Te_px, Te_py, Te_pz, Te_s ⚙️ 46 params 🗜 1211× vs SIESTA

Band structure — DFT (grey) vs TB H(k) (green)

Hamiltonian compression — symbolic TB vs SIESTA

1211×parameter compression
SIESTA H(k): 236 spinor orbitals (DZP) → dense 55696-entry matrix at every k.
TB H(k): 34 orbitals · 46 symbolic parameters, analytic in k.
Basis reduction 3.5× (118 → 34 orbitals).

Fermi surface — TB H(k) vs SIESTA DFT (H, S)

Green = our orthogonal TB; grey = the Fermi contour computed directly from SIESTA's own non-orthogonal Hamiltonian (read from the HSX). The overlap is the validation.

H(k) — the winner Hamiltonian

$$ H_{ij}(\mathbf{k}) \;=\; \varepsilon_i\,\delta_{ij}\;+\;\sum_{\mathbf{R}} w_{ij}^{\mathbf{R}}\,V_{\mathrm{key}}\,e^{\,i\,\mathbf{k}\cdot(\mathbf{R}\mathbf{A}+\boldsymbol{\tau}_j-\boldsymbol{\tau}_i)} $$

εi = on-site orbital energy (eV) · Vkey = Slater–Koster two-centre integral (eV) · w = symmetry-fixed angular coefficient · τ = orbital positions · A = real-space cell.

Br2Sc2Te2 (pseudo-Pmmn D2h) — H(k) (T4, species-shared on-sites, gap-focused)


Ideal Pmmn (#59); Sc1=Sc2,Te1=Te2,Br1=Br2; orthorhombic px≠py≠pz, per-orbital d. Kramers-paired (SOC inversion-protected). gap 0.419 eV indirect (VBM Γ, CBM Γ→Y). Scorecard: results/winner_score.json.

Notes & symmetry reasoning

Br₂Sc₂Te₂ (pseudo-Pmmn) — Br2Sc2Te2-ea0de383df8a — tight-binding model


Symbolic tight-binding H(k) reproducing the SIESTA DFT bands. Full evolution in

[notebook.md](notebook.md); method in the project [docs/workflow.md](../../docs/workflow.md).

Fifth material — the first low-symmetry (P1-relaxed), first heavy-SOC, and

densest-manifold case. Notably, the hardest material gave the cleanest scorecard.


TL;DR — winning model


T4 (Sc 4s,4p,3d + Te 4s,5p + Br 4s,4p; species-shared on-sites under the ideal

Pmmn (D₂h) symmetry), combined gap-focused fit. 34 bands, 46 params.


indirect gapVBM (Γ)CBM (Γ→Y)electron masshole massin-window [−2,2]
**0.6 meV** ✅**3.3 meV** ✅**2.6 meV** ✅**3.4%** ✅**5.6%** ✅64 meV

Every property in tolerance on the first gap-fit — no 2nd-ζ loop needed. Portable,

Γ-verified export in [tb_model/](tb_model/) (2.8×10⁻¹⁴ eV vs the engine).


Structure & symmetry — the pseudo-symmetry call


  • 6-atom Br–Te–Sc–Sc–Te–Br stack (a Sc₂ bilayer, Te+Br terminated). Orthorhombic,

    • |a|=5.802 ≠ |b|=3.989 Å, all 90°.

  • spglib at tight symprec=1e-3 returns P1 (#1) — no symmetry — but this is

    • relaxation noise: loosening symprec gives P-1 → P2₁/m → Pmmn (#59, D₂h) by

    3e-2. The ideal symmetry makes Sc₁≡Sc₂, Te₁≡Te₂, Br₁≡Br₂.

  • Modeling decision: share on-sites by species (the Pmmn equivalence) on the

    • real (P1) atomic positions — the phases stay exact, the parameter count halves

    (46 not ~90). Orthorhombic ⇒ p_x≠p_y≠p_z, 5 distinct d on-sites (per-orbital).

  • DFT basis (ORB_INDX): 118 orbitals (DZP); Te-5p and Sc-3d double-ζ.
  • 236 bands = clean Kramers pairs (0.64 meV intra-pair vs 3177 between): SOC is

    • present (heavy Te) but inversion-protected — it does not split the k-bands, so the

    target is a 118-band spinless set; λL·S would only add on-site multiplet splittings.

  • Indirect gap ≈ 0.42 eV, VBM at Γ (Te-5p), CBM along Γ→Y (Sc-3d) — Sc³⁺ is d⁰,

    • so it is a p→d gap (like TiSe₂) but in a denser (~18 near-E_F bands), heavier-SOC,

    lower-symmetry setting.


    Theory ladder (TB over SIESTA)


    theorybasisbandsparamsnear-gap
    T1Sc-3d + Te,Br-p2221167 meV
    T2+ Sc-4s2425151 meV
    T3+ Sc-4p3034138 meV
    **T4**+ Te,Br-s3446**105 meV** ★
    T4 + combined gap-fit(multi-seed, [−2,2])3446**64 in-window** ★★ winner

    PySR — span diagnostic + discovered formulas


  • VBM (Te-5p): span ~45 meV — Sc–Te pdπ (shell 2) + Br–Te ppσ: Te-5p hybridised

    • with both Sc-d and Br.

  • CBM (Sc-3d): span ~46 meV — a combination (Sc–Te sd + Sc–Te pdσ + Br–Sc pdπ),

    • no single dominant hopping.

  • Both spans plateau ~45 meV (vs TiSe₂'s 5/12). In this **dense, entangled manifold

    • no band is truly isolated**, so the single-band span is inherently less clean — read

    it as a property of the *diagnostic*, not a basis limit (the actual fit nails the

    gap/edges/masses). The CBM being a multi-coupling combination is the regime PySR's

    form-search is actually built for — richer than the single-lever clean materials,

    though the deliverable here did not *need* it. See results/pysr_formulas.json.


    Scope (precise): the winning H(k) is the Slater-Koster parameter-fit, **PySR-

    *guided*, not PySR-*modified*.**


    Why this hard case came out clean


    1. Pseudo-symmetry reduction — modeling with the ideal Pmmn equivalence (3 atoms,

    not 6) on the real positions kept it a 46-param, well-conditioned fit.

    2. Light, dispersive band edges — m_e 0.27, m_h 0.06 (NOT the flat, heavy bands that

    triggered the minimal-basis heavy-mass limit on TiSe₂/Ti₂CO₂). The heavy-mass limit

    is a property of band-edge *curvature*, not of being a d-material.

    3. SOC inversion-protected — Kramers pairs collapse to a clean spinless target;

    heavy-Te SOC did not have to be fit to reproduce the k-bands.


    How it compares to the literature


    Low-symmetry ternary Sc halide-tellurides have no standard TB model. Ours is

    per-material-faithful, symbolic, property-validated, with the pseudo-symmetry handled

    explicitly and SOC accounted for by Kramers analysis. The PySR formulas add

    interpretability.

  • [results/winner_score.json](results/winner_score.json), [results/pysr_formulas.json](results/pysr_formulas.json).
  • [tb_model/](tb_model/) — portable H(k) (params + formula + standalone use_tb.py).

    Hamiltonian compression — symbolic TB vs SIESTA

    901×parameter compression
    SIESTA H(k): 52 spinor orbitals (DZP) → dense 2704-entry matrix at every k.
    TB H(k): 2 orbitals · 3 symbolic parameters, analytic in k.
    Basis reduction 13.0× (26 → 2 orbitals).

    Fermi surface — TB H(k) vs SIESTA DFT (H, S)

    Green = our orthogonal TB; grey = the Fermi contour computed directly from SIESTA's own non-orthogonal Hamiltonian (read from the HSX). The overlap is the validation.

    H(k) — the winner Hamiltonian

    $$ H_{ij}(\mathbf{k}) \;=\; \varepsilon_i\,\delta_{ij}\;+\;\sum_{\mathbf{R}} w_{ij}^{\mathbf{R}}\,V_{\mathrm{key}}\,e^{\,i\,\mathbf{k}\cdot(\mathbf{R}\mathbf{A}+\boldsymbol{\tau}_j-\boldsymbol{\tau}_i)} $$

    εi = on-site orbital energy (eV) · Vkey = Slater–Koster two-centre integral (eV) · w = symmetry-fixed angular coefficient · τ = orbital positions · A = real-space cell.

    Graphene C2 (P6/mmm) — π (pz) Dirac model.


    Honeycomb, 2 C (A=B sublattice). pz only, 4 params. Nearest-neighbour t=-2.360 eV. Dirac cone at K. Scorecard: results/winner_score.json

    H(k) — analytic form

    Phase convention (identical to tb_core and use_tb.py):

    H_ij(k) = ε_i · δ_ij                                  (on-site)
        + Σ_R  w_ij^R · V_key · e^{ i k·(R·A + τ_j − τ_i) }   (Slater-Koster hop)
  • ε_i — on-site orbital energies (E_<class>_<orbital>), shared by symmetry class.
  • V_key — fitted SK two-centre radial integrals, one per <integral>_<class-pair>_s<shell>; independent per shell.
  • w_ij^R — the exact SK angular coefficient for bond (i→j, R), stored in params.json (w); the model never hand-codes phases.
  • A — the real-space cell; τ — orbital positions (real, set the Bloch phases).

    • 2 orbitals → 2 bands · 4 parameters (1 on-site, 3 hopping).


    On-site energies (eV)

    keyvalueterms
    `E_C_pz`+0.285122

    Slater-Koster hopping integrals (eV)

    keyvaluebonds
    `Vppp_CC_s1`-2.359843
    `Vppp_CC_s2`+0.326516
    `Vppp_CC_s3`+0.096273

    Notes & symmetry reasoning

    Graphene (C₂) — H(k) report


    Deliverable: tb_model/ — the canonical π (pz) tight-binding model of monolayer graphene.

    Type: Dirac semimetal (π bands touch at K).


    Structure & symmetry

    P6/mmm (#191), honeycomb, a=b=2.467 Å, 2 C atoms (A=B sublattice, one shared on-site).


    Model

  • Orbital set: C-2pz only (the π manifold), 2 bands. Both atoms share E_C_pz.
  • Hoppings: Slater–Koster Vppπ over 3 neighbour shells (4 params total).
  • The σ (s,pₓ,p_y) bands sit −3…−20 eV from E_F and are deliberately out of scope — they do not

    • affect the Dirac physics.


    Scorecard (Dirac semimetal)

    quantityDFTTBerror
    **Fermi velocity** (Dirac cone slope)12.62 eV·Å12.99 eV·Å**3 %**
    Dirac-point gap (closest path approach)18.6 meV18.5 meV✅
    nearest-neighbour hopping t—**−2.36 eV**consistent with textbook −2.7 eV

    Γ-verified 2.7×10⁻¹⁵ eV. Full-π-band RMSE 684 meV (band edges far from E_F; the Dirac region is exact).


    Takeaway: the orthogonal pz model reproduces graphene's defining physics — a Dirac cone with the

    correct Fermi velocity — with 4 symbolic parameters.

    Hamiltonian compression — symbolic TB vs SIESTA

    456×parameter compression
    SIESTA H(k): 154 spinor orbitals (DZP) → dense 23716-entry matrix at every k.
    TB H(k): 30 orbitals · 52 symbolic parameters, analytic in k.
    Basis reduction 2.6× (77 → 30 orbitals).

    Fermi surface — TB H(k) vs SIESTA DFT (H, S)

    Green = our orthogonal TB; grey = the Fermi contour computed directly from SIESTA's own non-orthogonal Hamiltonian (read from the HSX). The overlap is the validation.

    H(k) — the winner Hamiltonian

    $$ H_{ij}(\mathbf{k}) \;=\; \varepsilon_i\,\delta_{ij}\;+\;\sum_{\mathbf{R}} w_{ij}^{\mathbf{R}}\,V_{\mathrm{key}}\,e^{\,i\,\mathbf{k}\cdot(\mathbf{R}\mathbf{A}+\boldsymbol{\tau}_j-\boldsymbol{\tau}_i)} $$

    εi = on-site orbital energy (eV) · Vkey = Slater–Koster two-centre integral (eV) · w = symmetry-fixed angular coefficient · τ = orbital positions · A = real-space cell.

    Ti2CO2 MXene — H(k) (T3, symmetry-correct C3v d-splitting, gap-focused)


    P-3m1; Ti C3v d-split a1+2e; gap 0.282 eV indirect (VBM Γ, CBM M). Scorecard: results/winner_score.json.

    H(k) — analytic form

    Phase convention (identical to tb_core and use_tb.py):

    H_ij(k) = ε_i · δ_ij                                  (on-site)
        + Σ_R  w_ij^R · V_key · e^{ i k·(R·A + τ_j − τ_i) }   (Slater-Koster hop)
  • ε_i — on-site orbital energies (E_<class>_<orbital>), shared by symmetry class.
  • V_key — fitted SK two-centre radial integrals, one per <integral>_<class-pair>_s<shell>; independent per shell.
  • w_ij^R — the exact SK angular coefficient for bond (i→j, R), stored in params.json (w); the model never hand-codes phases.
  • A — the real-space cell; τ — orbital positions (real, set the Bloch phases).

    • 30 orbitals → 30 bands · 76 parameters (12 on-site, 64 hopping).

    Notes & symmetry reasoning

    Ti₂CO₂ MXene (CO2Ti2-c5bb3a35a784) — tight-binding model


    Symbolic tight-binding H(k) reproducing the SIESTA DFT bands. Full evolution

    (incl. failed theories, kept) in [notebook.md](notebook.md); method in the project

    [README.md](../../README.md) playbook. Third material (after ZnS, GaS) — the first

    transition-metal, d-manifold, ternary case.


    TL;DR — winning model


    spdp 5NN (T3: Ti 4s,4p,3d + C 2s,2p + O 2s,2p), symmetry-correct C₃ᵥ d-splitting,

    fit by a combined gap-focused optimization (seeded from all three ladder

    theories; window [−2,+2]). 30 bands.


    in-window [−2,2]indirect gapband edges (VBM/CBM)hole masselectron mass
    **71 meV****0.5 meV****2.9 / 2.4 meV**0.016 ✅**0.62 ✗ (basis-limited)**

    Portable, verified export in [tb_model/](tb_model/) (7×10⁻¹⁴ eV vs the engine).


    Structure & symmetry


  • 5-atom O–Ti–C–Ti–O sandwich (the classic MXene). Hexagonal,

    • space group P‑3̄m1 (#164), point group D₃d (spglib). Ti1≡Ti2 (Wyckoff 2d),

    O1≡O2 (2d), C alone (1b). Ti site symmetry C₃ᵥ ⇒ the 3d manifold splits into

    a₁(d_z²) + e(d_yz,d_zx) + e(d_xy,d_x²−y²) → 3 on-site energies (imposed),

    pₓ=p_y, and on-site a₁ hybridization allowed on Ti/O (not on the centrosymmetric C).

  • DFT basis (ORB_INDX): 77 orbitals (DZP) — Ti: 3s,4s,3p,4p,3d(×2ζ); C,O: 2s,2p,3d-pol.
  • Small indirect gap ≈ 0.28 eV inside a dense Ti-3d manifold (CBM at M, VBM at Γ).

    • The O-termination opens this gap in the otherwise-metallic Ti₂C. SOC negligible

    (Kramers split 0.1 meV). Literature-confirmed: VBM = C/O-2p, CBM = Ti-3d.


    Theory ladder (TB over SIESTA)


    theorybasisbandsnear-gapnote
    T1Ti(4s,3d)+C,O(2p)21162 meVminimal d-manifold + anion-p
    T2+ Ti-4p27146 meVcation-p helps (less than s/p-gap cases)
    **T3**+ C/O-2s30**136 meV**★ richest basis wins
    T3 + combined gap-fit(multi-seed, [−2,2])30**71 in-window**the winner
    T3 + 6NN, CBM-weightedmass-targeted30(e-mass 0.42)partial mass gain, traded gap — **kept**
    T3 + **2nd-ζ Ti-3d**40 bands40**gap inverts**FAILED — see below — **kept**

    PySR — genuine discovered formulas (the symbolic piece)


    On a single crystal V(d) is data-starved (no integral spans ≥3 shells), so PySR

    targeted the band formulas (where it has hundreds of k-points). The

    low-complexity (interpretable) end of the Pareto names the physics:

  • VBM ≈ 0.26·Re[V_ppπ(C–C)] − 2.09 → the valence top is the carbon π-network.
  • CBM ≈ −0.068·Re[V_ddσ(Ti–Ti)] + 0.77 (best 23 meV) → the conduction bottom

    • is Ti–Ti d-σ bonding.


    This is a real discovery — compact closed forms identifying the band-edge orbital

    character — and it guided the electron-mass campaign (CBM = Ti–Ti dσ → the d–d

    hopping is the curvature lever). See results/pysr_formulas.json.


    **Scope (be precise): the winning H(k) is the Slater-Koster parameter-fit,

    PySR-*guided*, NOT PySR-*modified*.** PySR discovered the band-edge *dispersions*

    E(k) (diagnostic) and pointed the lever; it did not author the H matrix elements

    (the winner's H is the SK ansatz with fitted constants). A genuinely PySR-modified

    H — a symbolic correction term δH_ij(k) discovered and scored *through the

    diagonalizer* — is the project frontier and is not yet done here.


    The electron mass — a basis-limit finding (kept negative results)


    The CBM is a flat, heavy Ti-3d band (m\*=0.90) the single-ζ model makes too light

    (0.275, err 0.62). Two PySR-guided attempts mapped the boundary:

    1. 6NN Ti–Ti d + heavy CBM-curvature weighting: moved it the right way

    (0.275 → 0.475, err 0.62 → 0.42) but only halfway, and traded gap accuracy

    (0.5 → 11 meV). Partially basis-limited.

    2. 2nd-ζ Ti-3d (the textbook fix; DFT basis is d×2ζ): the gap inverts — the

    dense 0.28 eV d-manifold cannot absorb 10 extra ζ2 bands via a warm-started fit;

    it needs a careful *constrained* fit, not a quick run. Optimization-limited atop

    basis-limited.


    Verdict (transferable): parallel to GaS's heavy hole mass — a flat, heavy

    band-edge mass is the minimal-basis failure mode, the effective mass is its

    diagnostic, and the 2nd-ζ fix is non-trivial in a dense small-gap manifold. The

    shipped winner keeps the excellent gap/edges/hole-mass; the heavy electron mass is

    the documented open item.


    How it compares to the literature


    SOTA for Ti₂CO₂ = DFT/GW (reference; GW opens the gap to ~1.3 eV, our PBE ref is

    0.28) and DFTB (generic pre-fit Slater-Koster; transferable but only approximately

    faithful). Ours is **per-material-faithful, automated, symbolic with the correct

    C₃ᵥ crystal-field grouping, property-validated**, and the PySR formulas add an

    interpretability the SOTA lacks. (MXenes are also a topological-insulator family —

    our λL·S capability is relevant for the SOC-opened TI members.)


    Artifacts

  • [results/bands_overlay.png](results/bands_overlay.png) — winner TB over SIESTA.
  • [results/bands_compare_ladder.png](results/bands_compare_ladder.png) — T1/T2/T3.
  • [results/dft_bands.png](results/dft_bands.png) — DFT bands.
  • [results/winner_score.json](results/winner_score.json), [results/pysr_formulas.json](results/pysr_formulas.json).
  • [tb_model/](tb_model/) — portable H(k) (params + standalone loader).

    • Update — k=1 valence defect fixed by increasing complexity (4→7 shells)


    QA found the 4-shell gap-fit invented spurious valence bands at k-path 1 (TB −0.75 eV vs DFT −3.0 eV):

    the gap-fit weighted out-of-window bands at only 0.05, leaving the deep valence unconstrained. Re-weighting

    failed (0.35 fixed the deep bands but broke m_e: 2.8 vs 0.9). The minimal basis lacked capacity, so we

    increased complexity: 4 → 7 neighbour shells (52 → 76 params). Result: **gap 0.0004 eV, m_e 3.7 %,

    m_h 4 %, in-window 72 meV, k=1 valence −2.75 eV (DFT −2.99) — spurious bands gone.** The earlier

    "heavy electron mass = basis limit" was actually a *shell-range* limit. Lesson: add capacity, don't juggle

    weights ([notes/lessons.md](../../notes/lessons.md)).

    Cl₂Ni₂Te₂ semimetal

    📐 P2₁/m (#11) 🧩 basis: Nia_dx2-y2, Nia_dxy, Nia_dyz, Nia_dz2, Nia_dzx, Nia_s, Tea_px, Tea_py, Tea_pz ⚙️ 26 params 🗜 1794× vs SIESTA

    Band structure — DFT (grey) vs TB H(k) (green)

    Computed scorecard (units shown)

    metricvalueunit
    theoryT1pNi4s_fermi_focused_v6
    near_ef_wrmse_meV559.1meV
    tight_ef_meV90.67meV
    ef_crossings_dft6
    ef_crossings_tb6
    fermi_velocity_dft6.936eV·Å
    fermi_velocity_tb6.453eV·Å
    v_err_pct6.961%

    Hamiltonian compression — symbolic TB vs SIESTA

    1794×parameter compression
    SIESTA H(k): 216 spinor orbitals (DZP) → dense 46656-entry matrix at every k.
    TB H(k): 18 orbitals · 26 symbolic parameters, analytic in k.
    Basis reduction 6.0× (108 → 18 orbitals).

    Fermi surface — TB H(k) vs SIESTA DFT (H, S)

    Green = our orthogonal TB; grey = the Fermi contour computed directly from SIESTA's own non-orthogonal Hamiltonian (read from the HSX). The overlap is the validation.

    H(k) — the winner Hamiltonian

    $$ H_{ij}(\mathbf{k}) \;=\; \varepsilon_i\,\delta_{ij}\;+\;\sum_{\mathbf{R}} w_{ij}^{\mathbf{R}}\,V_{\mathrm{key}}\,e^{\,i\,\mathbf{k}\cdot(\mathbf{R}\mathbf{A}+\boldsymbol{\tau}_j-\boldsymbol{\tau}_i)} $$

    εi = on-site orbital energy (eV) · Vkey = Slater–Koster two-centre integral (eV) · w = symmetry-fixed angular coefficient · τ = orbital positions · A = real-space cell.

    Cl₂Ni₂Te₂ (2D, P2₁/m #11, Janus Ni-telluride) — H(k) (T1+Ni4s, Fermi-focused v6).


    P2₁/m (#11, 2/m, with inversion); near-orthorhombic (a≠b, γ≈90°). Ni₂ + Te₂ + Cl₂. METAL: 6 E_F crossings, Ni-3d+Ni-4s+Te-5p at E_F. Kramers-collapsed spinless target. SOC enters as on-site λL·S.

    H(k) — analytic form

    Phase convention (identical to tb_core and use_tb.py):

    H_ij(k) = ε_i · δ_ij                                  (on-site)
        + Σ_R  w_ij^R · V_key · e^{ i k·(R·A + τ_j − τ_i) }   (Slater-Koster hop)
  • ε_i — on-site orbital energies (E_<class>_<orbital>), shared by symmetry class.
  • V_key — fitted SK two-centre radial integrals, one per <integral>_<class-pair>_s<shell>; independent per shell.
  • w_ij^R — the exact SK angular coefficient for bond (i→j, R), stored in params.json (w); the model never hand-codes phases.
  • A — the real-space cell; τ — orbital positions (real, set the Bloch phases).

    • 18 orbitals → 18 bands · 26 parameters (9 on-site, 17 hopping).


    On-site energies (eV)

    keyvalueterms
    `E_Nia_dx2-y2`-1.334412
    `E_Nia_dxy`-2.605082
    `E_Nia_dyz`-0.521092
    `E_Nia_dz2`-1.310302
    `E_Nia_dzx`-4.084192
    `E_Nia_s`-2.176822
    `E_Tea_px`-1.185802
    `E_Tea_py`-1.158002
    `E_Tea_pz`-1.489592

    Slater-Koster hopping integrals (eV)

    keyvaluebonds
    `Vddd_NiaNia_s3`+0.1012126
    `Vddp_NiaNia_s3`-0.5257816
    `Vdds_NiaNia_s3`-0.0378818
    `Vpdp_NiaTea_s1`-0.0641560
    `Vpdp_NiaTea_s2`+1.4805560
    `Vpds_NiaTea_s1`-0.7492160
    `Vpds_NiaTea_s2`+0.0215560
    `Vppp_TeaTea_s3`+0.6249010
    `Vppp_TeaTea_s4`-0.0114527
    `Vppp_TeaTea_s5`+0.257639
    `Vpps_TeaTea_s3`+0.539228
    `Vpps_TeaTea_s4`+0.6234527
    `Vpps_TeaTea_s5`-5.041199
    `Vsd_NiaNia_s3`+0.0246012
    `Vsp_NiaTea_s1`+0.1422712
    `Vsp_NiaTea_s2`+0.1622012
    `Vss_NiaNia_s3`+0.399682

    Notes & symmetry reasoning

    Cl₂Ni₂Te₂ — H(k) report


    Material: Cl₂Ni₂Te₂ (Janus Ni-telluride dichloride), 2D monolayer, DB hash 3133f6a8ada2.

    Deliverable: materials/Cl2Ni2Te2-3133f6a8ada2/tb_model/ — params.json + formula.md + use_tb.py, Γ-verified.



    1. Structure & symmetry


    PropertyValue
    Cella=5.414, b=3.496, c=21.342 Å, γ≈90° (near-orthorhombic)
    AtomsNi₂ + Te₂ + Cl₂ (6 total)
    Space groupP2₁/m (#11), point group 2/m, **with inversion**
    Equivalent atoms2 Ni, 2 Te, 2 Cl (inversion partners)
    Parameter classes3 (Nia, Tea, Cla) — share by species
    Ni siteC_i → 5 distinct d on-sites (orthorhombic a≠b)

    Symmetry (spglib-verified, symprec 1e-2): space group P2₁/m (#11), point group 2/m (C₂h), 4 operations, inversion present — independently re-checked on aiida.STRUCT_OUT (Z=[Ni,Ni,Te,Te,Cl,Cl]). Although Te and Cl sit on opposite faces (a "Janus"-like decoration), the relaxed structure *retains inversion* (it is centrosymmetric, not a symmetry-breaking Janus). This is the load-bearing fact for the model: inversion protects Kramers degeneracy, so SOC does not split the k-bands (it only splits on-site multiplets). Parameters are shared by species (Nia, Tea, Cla).


    2. DFT reference


  • Engine: SIESTA, PBEsol, SOC (fully relativistic)
  • Basis: Ni DZP (19 orb/atom), Te DZP (22 orb/atom), Cl DZ (13 orb/atom) → 108 spinor orbitals
  • Bands: 216 spinor → 108 spinless (Kramers-collapsed, inversion-protected, max splitting <0.05 eV)
  • Metal: bands 13 & 14 cross E_F = −4.439 eV, 6 crossings total
  • Fermi velocity: 6.94 eV·Å

    • 3. Theory ladder


    All theories use 5 shells, class-sharing by species, per-orbital on-sites, SK-validated hopping, shell-resolved integrals.


    TheoryBasisBandsParamsnear-E_F wRMSEKept?
    T1Ni(3d) + Te(5p)1621171 meV✅ best ladder
    T2T1 + Cl(3p)2226210 meV❌ Cl-3p deep, degrades
    T3T2 + Ni(4s,4p)3046132 meV❌ over-parameterised for E_F
    T4T3 + Te(5s) + Cl(3s)3460crashed❌ too many params

    Key finding: Cl-3p lies >2 eV below E_F. Adding it dilutes the near-E_F fit without helping the Fermi surface. The E_F physics is Ni-3d + Te-5p with Ni-4s providing a light dispersive channel.


    4. Fermi-focused model (final)


    Reduced basis: Ni(3d + 4s) + Te(5p) — 18 bands, 26 params.

    Method: scipy.least_squares with hard E_F weighting (×6 in |E|<0.5 eV, ×2 in |E|<1.5 eV, ×0.15 outside) + explicit velocity residual at DFT crossing points (velocity weight = 16).


    PySR diagnostic

    Single-band span on E_F-crossing bands 13 & 14: 36–39 meV RMSE, no plateau → OPT-limited. The SK two-centre form spans the near-E_F bands — the basis is adequate, the residual error is an optimization tradeoff.


    Final scorecard


    PropertyDFTTB modelError
    E_F crossings66**0**
    Fermi velocity6.94 eV·Å6.45 eV·Å**7.0%**
    tight-E_F RMSE (E<0.6 eV)—91 meV—
    near-E_F wRMSE (σ=2 eV)—559 meV—

    Γ-verification

    Export vs engine maximum deviation at Γ: 1.7×10⁻¹⁴ eV — bit-identical.


    5. Iteration history (kept failures)


    VersionMethodCrossingsv_errOutcome
    v1hard E_F weight, 30 starts6/621%baseline
    v2improved ranking (vel ×60)6/620.3%reference point
    v3soft Gaussian (σ=0.4)6/636.8%❌ diluted E_F focus
    v4hard cutoff, 50 starts, escalate to σ=0.66/621.6%same plateau as v2
    v5velocity residual (vw=10, best rank)8/624.8%❌ broke crossing topology
    **v6****velocity residual grid search, vw=16****6/6****7.0%**✅ **ACCEPTED**

    The decisive move was adding explicit velocity residual terms to least_squares (not just post-hoc ranking). The velocity weight has a narrow sweet spot: vw=14–16 yields 6 crossings + v_err 7–9%; vw<6 gives little improvement; vw>18 creates spurious crossings.


    6. Limitations & extensions


  • SOC (λL·S): Inversion symmetry protects Kramers degeneracy — SOC does NOT split k-bands. It splits on-site multiplets (Δ_SO). λL·S is the next extension for spin-dependent properties.
  • Broad-window RMSE: 559 meV — the far bands (>2 eV from E_F) are poorly constrained. This is the expected tradeoff of a Fermi-focused fit.
  • 2nd-ζ orbitals: Not needed — PySR confirms the basis spans the E_F bands (OPT-limited, not basis-limited).
  • Cl orbitals: Spectator at E_F. The Janus Cl-face is structurally important but electronically inert at the Fermi level.

    • 7. Deliverable


    materials/Cl2Ni2Te2-3133f6a8ada2/tb_model/
├── params.json    — 26 parameters, site positions, hopping table with SK angular weights
├── formula.md     — H(k) analytic form, parameter table, phase convention
├── use_tb.py      — standalone loader: H(k), bands(), no engine dependency

    Load and use:

    import sys; sys.path.insert(0, 'tb_model/')
import use_tb
m = use_tb.load()
H = use_tb.hamiltonian(m, [0.0, 0.0, 0.0])   # H(Γ)
bands = use_tb.bands(m, kpath)                 # bands along a path

    Γ-verified: max|use_tb.bands − engine.bands| = 1.7×10⁻¹⁴ eV at k=(0,0,0).

    Cr₂Se₄ metal

    📐 — 🧩 basis: Cr_dx2-y2, Cr_dxy, Cr_dyz, Cr_dz2, Cr_dzx, Cr_px, Cr_py, Cr_pz, Cr_s, Se_px, Se_py, Se_pz, Se_s ⚙️ 62 params 🗜 —× vs SIESTA

    Band structure — DFT (grey) vs TB H(k) (green)

    Computed scorecard (units shown)

    metricvalueunit
    theoryCr_spd_Se_sp_6shell_velmatched
    near_ef_wrmse_meV312meV
    tight_ef_meV69.02meV
    ef_crossings_dft5
    ef_crossings_tb5
    fermi_velocity_dft2.203eV·Å
    fermi_velocity_tb2.146eV·Å
    velocity_err_pct2.554%

    Fermi surface — TB H(k) vs SIESTA DFT (H, S)

    Green = our orthogonal TB; grey = the Fermi contour computed directly from SIESTA's own non-orthogonal Hamiltonian (read from the HSX). The overlap is the validation.

    H(k) — the winner Hamiltonian

    $$ H_{ij}(\mathbf{k}) \;=\; \varepsilon_i\,\delta_{ij}\;+\;\sum_{\mathbf{R}} w_{ij}^{\mathbf{R}}\,V_{\mathrm{key}}\,e^{\,i\,\mathbf{k}\cdot(\mathbf{R}\mathbf{A}+\boldsymbol{\tau}_j-\boldsymbol{\tau}_i)} $$

    εi = on-site orbital energy (eV) · Vkey = Slater–Koster two-centre integral (eV) · w = symmetry-fixed angular coefficient · τ = orbital positions · A = real-space cell.

    Cr2Se4 (P2_1/m C2h) - H(k). METAL.

    Cr(s,p,d)+Se(s,p), 6 shells, 62 params, velocity-matched. tight-E_F 69 meV.

    H(k) — analytic form

    Phase convention (identical to tb_core and use_tb.py):

    H_ij(k) = ε_i · δ_ij                                  (on-site)
        + Σ_R  w_ij^R · V_key · e^{ i k·(R·A + τ_j − τ_i) }   (Slater-Koster hop)
  • ε_i — on-site orbital energies (E_<class>_<orbital>), shared by symmetry class.
  • V_key — fitted SK two-centre radial integrals, one per <integral>_<class-pair>_s<shell>; independent per shell.
  • w_ij^R — the exact SK angular coefficient for bond (i→j, R), stored in params.json (w); the model never hand-codes phases.
  • A — the real-space cell; τ — orbital positions (real, set the Bloch phases).

    • 34 orbitals → 34 bands · 62 parameters (13 on-site, 49 hopping).

    Notes & symmetry reasoning

    Cr₂Se₄ — TB Hamiltonian H(k) over SIESTA bands


    Structure: Cr₂Se₄, space group P2₁/m (C₂ₕ, monoclinic, low symmetry). Metal — 5 bands cross

    E_F. Cr is the magnetic ion in the bulk chalcogenide family, but this DFT reference is the

    non-magnetic database entry; treat as a metal and fit the Fermi-surface topology + Fermi velocity.


    Theory ladder (TB over SIESTA — failures kept)


    theorybasisshellsparamstight-E_F RMSEE_F crossingsv_F errorverdict
    T1 `Cr3d_Se4p`Cr d + Se p4~40high0/5—✗ misses the Fermi surface entirely
    T2 `Cr3d4s_Se4p`Cr s,d + Se p5~45**178 meV****2/5****87 %**✗ "lazy" — passes a coarse 2-crossing gate but **misses 3 of 5 crossings and the Fermi velocity is 87 % off**
    **T3 `Cr_spd_Se_sp_6shell_velmatched`****Cr s,p,d + Se s,p****6****62****69 meV****5/5 ✓****3 % ✓****✓ winner**

    Why T2 was not actually a winner. It cleared a crossings-count gate (2 crossings present) while a

    key physical metric — the Fermi velocity — was off by 87 %, and it captured only 2 of the 5 DFT

    Fermi-surface sheets. That is the *first rung*, not the answer (see CLAUDE.md invariant #8). Climbing:


    1. Richer basis — added Cr 4p and Se 4s to the Cr-spd/Se-sp set (more hybridization freedom near

    E_F where the d–p mixing is strong).

    2. More neighbour shells (5 → 6) — orthogonal models spend hopping range to absorb the overlap

    (Löwdin); the extra shell closes the residual without an overlap matrix.

    3. Velocity-matched loss — an explicit residual on |dE/dk| at each DFT Fermi crossing, so the fit

    optimizes the *slope* (Fermi velocity), not just the band positions.

    4. 14 random restarts — the velocity-matched minimum is narrow; restart 12 found it.


    Result (winner): tight-E_F 69 meV, 5/5 Fermi crossings, Fermi velocity **2.15 vs 2.20

    eV·Å (2.6 % off)**, near-E_F weighted RMSE 312 meV (the low-symmetry monoclinic dispersion is dense).

    Γ-verified: max|use_tb.bands − engine.bands| = 3.6×10⁻¹⁴ over the full path. Orthogonal, S=I.


    Reproduce: python materials/Cr2Se4-27ef6952ebef/theories/run_cr2se4_lean.py

    Overlay: results/bands_final.png · exported model: tb_model/ (use_tb.py, formula.md).

    Hamiltonian compression — symbolic TB vs SIESTA

    589×parameter compression
    SIESTA H(k): 180 spinor orbitals (DZP) → dense 32400-entry matrix at every k.
    TB H(k): 34 orbitals · 55 symbolic parameters, analytic in k.
    Basis reduction 2.6× (90 → 34 orbitals).

    Fermi surface — TB H(k) vs SIESTA DFT (H, S)

    Green = our orthogonal TB; grey = the Fermi contour computed directly from SIESTA's own non-orthogonal Hamiltonian (read from the HSX). The overlap is the validation.

    H(k) — the winner Hamiltonian

    $$ H_{ij}(\mathbf{k}) \;=\; \varepsilon_i\,\delta_{ij}\;+\;\sum_{\mathbf{R}} w_{ij}^{\mathbf{R}}\,V_{\mathrm{key}}\,e^{\,i\,\mathbf{k}\cdot(\mathbf{R}\mathbf{A}+\boldsymbol{\tau}_j-\boldsymbol{\tau}_i)} $$

    εi = on-site orbital energy (eV) · Vkey = Slater–Koster two-centre integral (eV) · w = symmetry-fixed angular coefficient · τ = orbital positions · A = real-space cell.

    FeS2 (2D, P2_1/m C2h) — H(k) (T4, class-shared on-sites Fe/Sa/Sb, curvature-weighted)


    P2_1/m (#11); Fe1=Fe2, two inequivalent S (Sa,Sb); orthorhombic px!=py!=pz, per-orbital d. Kramers-paired (SOC inversion-protected). Fe2+ d6 => d->d gap 0.641 eV indirect (VBM/CBM off-symmetry on S-Y). Scorecard: results/winner_score.json.

    H(k) — analytic form

    Phase convention (identical to tb_core and use_tb.py):

    H_ij(k) = ε_i · δ_ij                                  (on-site)
        + Σ_R  w_ij^R · V_key · e^{ i k·(R·A + τ_j − τ_i) }   (Slater-Koster hop)
  • ε_i — on-site orbital energies (E_<class>_<orbital>), shared by symmetry class.
  • V_key — fitted SK two-centre radial integrals, one per <integral>_<class-pair>_s<shell>; independent per shell.
  • w_ij^R — the exact SK angular coefficient for bond (i→j, R), stored in params.json (w); the model never hand-codes phases.
  • A — the real-space cell; τ — orbital positions (real, set the Bloch phases).

    • 34 orbitals → 34 bands · 55 parameters (17 on-site, 38 hopping).

    `Vppp_FeaSb_s3`-1.5323636
    `Vppp_FeaSb_s4`-0.4670214
    `Vpps_FeaFea_s5`-1.6648718
    `Vpps_FeaSa_s1`-2.942178
    `Vpps_FeaSa_s2`+0.9728636
    `Vpps_FeaSb_s3`+4.7095636
    `Vpps_FeaSb_s4`+1.8534912
    `Vsd_FeaFea_s5`-2.7970020
    `Vsd_FeaSa_s1`+0.290536
    `Vsd_FeaSa_s2`-0.5542320
    `Vsd_FeaSb_s3`-1.0799520
    `Vsd_FeaSb_s4`-0.940178
    `Vsp_FeaFea_s5`+0.7342612
    `Vsp_FeaSa_s1`-1.302928
    `Vsp_FeaSa_s2`+0.0481024
    `Vsp_FeaSb_s3`-0.3346724
    `Vsp_FeaSb_s4`+0.8535710
    `Vss_FeaFea_s5`-0.021852
    `Vss_FeaSa_s1`+4.810762
    `Vss_FeaSa_s2`+0.701194
    `Vss_FeaSb_s3`-1.783974
    `Vss_FeaSb_s4`+0.049262

    Notes & symmetry reasoning

    FeS₂ — 2D P2₁/m (Fe2S4-ae77a95fa237) — tight-binding model


    Symbolic tight-binding H(k) reproducing the SIESTA DFT bands. Full evolution (incl. the

    kept/failed theories) in [notebook.md](notebook.md); method in the project

    [docs/workflow.md](../../docs/workflow.md). Sixth material — the **correlated Fe-3d (Fe²⁺

    d⁶)** case, where the gap lives *inside* the d-manifold and symbolic regression was predicted

    to matter most.


    ⚠️ Structure: NOT bulk cubic pyrite


    The request was "FeS₂ (pyrite)", but the DB's relaxed structure is a 2D slab, |a|=3.202 ≠

    |b|=5.294 Å at 90°, P2₁/m (#11), point group 2/m (C₂h) — *not* the textbook cubic pyrite

    (Pa-3̄). The space group is stable across symprec 1e-3→1e-1 (genuinely P2₁/m, not

    pseudo-symmetric). All numbers below are for this 2D polymorph. (Fourth confirmation of the

    GaS/TiSe₂ lesson: verify the geometry, never the material's reputation.)


    TL;DR — winning model


    **T4 (Fe 4s,4p,3d + S 3s,3p; class-shared on-sites Fe/S_a/S_b), combined gap-focused fit then

    curvature-weighted refit.** 34 bands, 55 params, 5-shell cutoff.


    indirect gapVBMCBMhole masselectron massin-window [−2,2]
    **4.5 meV** ✅**4.5 meV** ✅**8.9 meV** ✅**5.7%** ✅27.5% ⚠~84 meV

    Gap, both band edges, and the hole mass pass every tolerance; the **electron mass (Fe-e_g\*

    pocket) lands 2.5 points over** the ~25% soft tolerance — the residual minimal-basis softness,

    reduced from 34%. Portable, Γ-verified export in [tb_model/](tb_model/).


    Structure & symmetry


  • 6 atoms = 2 Fe + 4 S (Fe₂S₄ = 2 FeS₂), orthorhombic 2D slab (c=17.68 Å vacuum). a≠b ⇒

    • p_x ≠ p_y.

  • Equivalent atoms [0,1,1,0,4,4]: Fe₁≡Fe₂ (class Fe), S₁≡S₂ (class S_a), S₃≡S₄

    • (class S_b) — one Fe orbit but TWO inequivalent S sublattices (different z-heights /

    S–S-dimer environments). On-sites/hops keyed by these 3 spglib classes, not by species

    (species-sharing would force a false S_a=S_b degeneracy).

  • Low C₂h site symmetry ⇒ p_x≠p_y≠p_z and 5 distinct (per-orbital) d on-sites — general

    • enough to carry the t₂g/e_g crystal-field split that sets the gap.

  • DFT basis (ORB_INDX): 90 orbitals (DZP) — Fe 3s,3p,3d(×2ζ),4s(×2ζ),4p; S

    • 3s(×2ζ),3p(×2ζ),3d. The double-ζ Fe-3d is the natural basis-limit lever if the d-masses

    stay heavy.

  • Bands: 180 spinor bands, Kramers split 0.10 meV (SOC, inversion-protected) →

    • collapsed to 90 spinless. Indirect gap 0.641 eV, **VBM & CBM both off-symmetry on

    the S–Y line (found by value, not at a high-symmetry point). Fe²⁺ low-spin d⁶ ⇒ a d→d

    gap** (filled t₂g → empty e_g\*) with S-3p in the valence — like Ti₂CO₂.


    Theory ladder (TB over SIESTA) — all kept


    theorybasis addedbandsparamsnear-gap wRMSEnote
    T1Fe-3d + S-3p2222203 meVminimal: d-manifold + anion-p
    T2+ Fe-4s2429166 meVcation-s
    T3+ Fe-4p3045138 meVcation-p
    **T4**+ S-3s3455**109 meV** ★anion-s completeness — best
    **T4 + gap-focused fit**window [−2,2], multi-seed3455**gap 1.2 meV**clean gap; masses 34%/35% light
    **T4 + curvature-weighted**+ effective-mass penalty3455**gap 4.5 meV** ★★**mass fix → balanced deliverable**

    Monotonic near-gap improvement T1→T4 ⇒ correct basis, optimization-bounded. (Whole-band RMSE

    *rises* at T3/T4 — the far unweighted bands degrade as the basis grows; irrelevant to the

    near-gap properties that are scored.) Both gap-fit models kept: the gap-focused one is the

    tightest-gap alternative, the curvature-weighted one is the property-complete winner.


    PySR — the span diagnostic + discovered formulas


    PySR genuinely ran (Julia backend). Single-band span on the winner's 38 Bloch features:


  • VBM (Fe-t₂g/S-3p): span → 17.6 meV, no plateau (110→44→18 meV across complexity).
  • CBM (Fe-e_g\*): span → 15.0 meV, no plateau (122→29→15 meV).

    • Verdict: OPT-LIMITED, basis adequate. Both edge bands are representable to ~15–18 meV in

    the existing orbital set, so the light masses were a loss-shaping miss (stride-4 sampling +

    no curvature term), not a missing-orbital one. *(I initially misread the first minutes of

    evolution — which sit at the c=1 constant ~110 meV — as basis-limited; the converged Pareto

    overturned it. Lesson recorded.)*


    Guidance — PySR named the curvature levers (the Pareto-reads-the-physics use):

  • VBM: the big drop at c=6 is cos(abs2_Vsd_FeaFea_s5) → the Fe–Fe s–d hop at shell 5

    • controls the VBM dispersion.

  • CBM: at c=3 abs2_Vddp_FeaFea_s5 → the Fe–Fe d–d(π) shell-5 hop, plus Fe–S V_ss /

    • V_ppσ terms.


    So the band-edge curvature is set by longer-range Fe–Fe (shell 5) + Fe–S couplings — the

    exact terms a stride-4, energy-only loss under-constrains. See

    [results/pysr_formulas.json](results/pysr_formulas.json).


    Scope (precise): the winning H(k) is the Slater-Koster parameter fit, **PySR-*guided*,

    not PySR-*modified*.** PySR discovered the band-edge dispersions E(k) (diagnostic) and named

    the lever; it did not author the H matrix elements.


    Was SR decisive here? Confirmatory + guiding, not irreplaceable. Despite FeS₂ being the

    correlated-d case flagged as high-PySR-impact, the bands turned out *representable* (opt-, not

    basis-limited): the same conclusion is reachable from the mass-plateau diagnostic (gap

    meV-exact but curvature wrong after the fit converged) plus chemical reasoning (edge curvature

    in a layered d-metal comes from the longer-range metal–metal hops). PySR **confirmed

    opt-limited, named the shell-5 Fe–Fe levers, and produced a falsifiable closed-form E(k)** —

    valuable, but the fix was reachable without it. SR's decisive, irreplaceable value remains

    expected on the metallic / entangled-d–p / no-clean-gap members (NiTe₂ archetype), where

    the mass diagnostic doesn't apply. Consistent with all clean-gap materials so far.


    The electron mass — fixed by loss-shaping, not new orbitals


    The gap-focused winner nailed the gap (1.2 meV) but left both band-edge masses **~34% too

    light** (edges too dispersive). Guided by PySR's opt-limited verdict, the fix was a

    curvature-weighted refit of the same T4 model (no new orbitals): full-path sampling (to

    resolve the 5-point edge parabola) + an explicit effective-mass penalty at VBM/CBM, with the

    winner chosen by joint tolerance violation so bands + gap + edges + both masses must pass

    *together*.


    Result: m_h\* 35% → 5.7%, m_e\* 34% → 27.5%, with gap held at 4.5 meV and edges

    ≤9 meV. A first, greedy mass-only selection reached m_e\* 9% but broke the gap to 8.4 meV — it

    was rejected by the joint-violation criterion. The residual gap↔m_e\* tension is real:

    the single-ζ basis reaches m_e\* 9% *or* gap 1.2 meV but not both below (25%, 5 meV) at once;

    the joint frontier passes through ~(27%, 4.5 meV).


    Decision — STOP (documented limit). The lever for the last 2.5 points of m_e\* is the DFT's

    2nd-ζ Fe-3d (radial freedom on the e_g\* band), but the 0.64 eV gap is borderline — between

    Ti₂CO₂ (0.28 eV, where 2nd-ζ *inverted* the gap) and TiSe₂ (0.77 eV, where it *worked*). Not

    worth risking the clean 4.5 meV gap for a marginal electron-pocket curvature. Documented per the

    iteration contract.


    How it compares to the literature


    Bulk-pyrite FeS₂ SOTA (Pa-3̄, the photovoltaic/thermoelectric absorber; DFT+U, GW, Wannier TB)

    does not apply — this DB entry is a *different, 2D P2₁/m* polymorph. Against generic DFTB

    (transferable but approximate), ours is **per-material-faithful, automated, symbolic with the

    correct C₂h class structure (two inequivalent S sublattices) and per-orbital d crystal field,

    property-validated**, with PySR-named band-edge physics the SOTA lacks.


    Artifacts


  • [results/bands_final.png](results/bands_final.png) — final exported H(k) vs SIESTA (rendered via tb_model/use_tb.py; full + near-gap, scorecard in title).
  • [results/bands_overlay.png](results/bands_overlay.png) — winner (curvature-weighted) TB over SIESTA, from the fit.
  • [results/bands_preview.png](results/bands_preview.png) — gap-focused winner over SIESTA.
  • [results/ladder_summary.json](results/ladder_summary.json) — T1–T4 ladder scores.
  • [results/winner_score.json](results/winner_score.json) — final property scorecard.
  • [results/pysr_formulas.json](results/pysr_formulas.json) — discovered VBM/CBM E(k) + span.
  • [tb_model/](tb_model/) — portable H(k) (params + formula + standalone use_tb.py), Γ-checked vs the engine.

    • Ga₂S₂ —

    📐 — 🧩 basis: Ga_dx2-y2, Ga_dxy, Ga_dyz, Ga_dz2, Ga_dzx, Ga_px, Ga_py, Ga_pz, Ga_s, S_px, S_py, S_pz ⚙️ 44 params 🗜 445× vs SIESTA

    Band structure — DFT (grey) vs TB H(k) (green)

    Computed scorecard (units shown)

    scorecard pending

    Hamiltonian compression — symbolic TB vs SIESTA

    445×parameter compression
    SIESTA H(k): 140 spinor orbitals (DZP) → dense 19600-entry matrix at every k.
    TB H(k): 24 orbitals · 44 symbolic parameters, analytic in k.
    Basis reduction 2.9× (70 → 24 orbitals).

    Fermi surface — TB H(k) vs SIESTA DFT (H, S)

    Green = our orthogonal TB; grey = the Fermi contour computed directly from SIESTA's own non-orthogonal Hamiltonian (read from the HSX). The overlap is the validation.

    H(k) — the winner Hamiltonian

    $$ H_{ij}(\mathbf{k}) \;=\; \varepsilon_i\,\delta_{ij}\;+\;\sum_{\mathbf{R}} w_{ij}^{\mathbf{R}}\,V_{\mathrm{key}}\,e^{\,i\,\mathbf{k}\cdot(\mathbf{R}\mathbf{A}+\boldsymbol{\tau}_j-\boldsymbol{\tau}_i)} $$

    εi = on-site orbital energy (eV) · Vkey = Slater–Koster two-centre integral (eV) · w = symmetry-fixed angular coefficient · τ = orbital positions · A = real-space cell.

    GaS — H(k) (spdp 5NN, p_x/p_y-resolved, COMBINED gap-weighted fit)


    Window [-3,3] eV, gap-emphasised but whole-window kept. Gap 1.944 eV (indirect, VBM at zone boundary). Scorecard: results/combined_score.json.

    Notes & symmetry reasoning

    GaS monolayer (Ga2S2-2e534b47f40c) — tight-binding model


    Symbolic tight-binding H(k) reproducing the SIESTA DFT bands. The Hamiltonian is

    the deliverable; the bands/properties are the score. Full evolution in

    [notebook.md](notebook.md); method in the project [README.md](../../README.md)

    playbook. Second material processed (after ZnS).


    TL;DR — winning model


    spdp 5NN, pₓ/p_y-resolved on-sites (orthogonal, real orbitals, 24 bands, 44

    params), fit by a combined optimization: seeded from both a general and a

    gap-focused solution, loss weighted heavily on the gap but keeping the whole

    [−3,+3] eV window.


    in-window [−3,3]indirect gapband edges (VBM/CBM)electron masshole mass
    **58 meV****0.6 meV****4.8 / 5.4 meV**0.063→0.069 (**9 %**)−0.64→−0.15 (**open**)

    Portable, verified export in [tb_model/](tb_model/) (4×10⁻¹⁴ eV vs the engine).


    Structure & symmetry — NOT textbook hexagonal GaS


  • Puckered S–Ga–Ga–S slab, but the cell is oblique: a = 3.646, b = 5.256 Å,

    • angle 110.3° (|a| ≠ |b|; hexagonal needs a = b, 120°). Lower symmetry than the

    P‑6̄m2 hexagonal GaS of the literature → pₓ ≠ p_y (per-orbital on-sites; the

    hexagonal "in-plane" grouping is wrong here). S1≡S2, Ga1≡Ga2 by the inversion

    centre.

  • DFT basis (ORB_INDX): 70 orbitals (DZP) — S: 3s(×2ζ),3p(×2ζ),3d(pol);

    • Ga: 3s,4s(×2ζ),3p,4p(×2ζ),3d,4d. (Our model carries the first-ζ valence subset.)

  • Band topology (verified, not assumed): indirect gap 1.94 eV, CBM at Γ.

    • The III–VI Mexican hat is present but QUENCHED (~49 meV) near Γ (Γ is a local

    minimum; ring max at kd≈0.11); the **true VBM is at a zone-boundary high-symmetry

    point** (−1.05 eV), not the Γ ring. The oblique distortion reshaped the textbook

    valence topology. See results/valence_analysis.png.


    Theory ladder (TB over SIESTA)


    theorywhatin-window [−3,3]gape-massh-mass
    general (spdp, Fermi-weighted whole near-gap)24 bands, 44 params136 meV———
    gap-focused (window [−5,4] + gap/edge penalties)warm-started102 meV1 meV~2× off0.51
    **combined (seed BOTH, [−3,3], gap-weighted + balanced, 100 starts)**—**58 meV****0.6 meV****9 %**0.49 ★

    Why combined wins: seeding from both solutions and keeping weight across the

    whole window (while emphasising the gap) **recovered the electron mass the pure

    gap-fit had sacrificed** (~2× → 9 %), with no loss of gap/edge accuracy — a

    multi-objective balance that beats either extreme. (Running-best stepped

    77→63→58 meV by restart 20; flat thereafter.)


    Physical interpretation


  • Gap & character: CBM at Γ (Ga-network conduction), VBM at a zone boundary on

    • the S-3p valence manifold; indirect 1.94 eV gap, reproduced to <1 meV.

  • The Mexican hat is a shallow (~49 meV) Γ ring, quenched by the oblique

    • distortion — captured qualitatively but it is not the band maximum here.

  • Open item — the heavy hole mass. DFT hole mass −0.64 (flat, heavy,

    • near-divergent zone-boundary VBM) vs TB −0.15. This is a basis limitation: a

    minimal single-ζ s/p/d set cannot bend a band that flat. Energies are near-exact

    while the *curvature* is not — the effective mass is the diagnostic. The fix

    (next): the physical orbitals SIESTA used (S-3d/3s, second-ζ), confirmed by the

    single-band PySR span test, and/or heavy VBM-curvature weighting.


    How it compares to the literature


    The SOTA III–VI TB models (Fal'ko group, s/p 16-band even/odd; k·p Ak²−Bk⁴)

    assume hexagonal D₃ₕ GaS/GaSe/InSe — they do not describe this oblique

    structure, and the s/p models omit d (failing away from the edges). Our model is

    d-inclusive, full-BZ, automated, SOC-ready, property-validated, and is the

    correct per-structure treatment for this lower-symmetry cell. Wannier still wins on

    raw whole-band accuracy; the established k·p still gives a cleaner analytic hat near

    Γ (which here is shallow anyway).


    Artifacts

  • [results/bands_overlay.png](results/bands_overlay.png) — TB (magenta) over SIESTA (grey).
  • [results/valence_analysis.png](results/valence_analysis.png) — Mexican-hat / zone-boundary VBM analysis.
  • [results/combined_score.json](results/combined_score.json) — the scorecard above.
  • [tb_model/](tb_model/) — portable H(k) (params + formula + standalone loader).

    • In₂S₂ semiconductor

    📐 P1 (#1) 🧩 basis: In_dx2-y2, In_dxy, In_dyz, In_dz2, In_dzx, In_px, In_py, In_pz, In_s, S_px, S_py, S_pz, S_s ⚙️ 51 params 🗜 384× vs SIESTA

    Band structure — DFT (grey) vs TB H(k) (green)

    Computed scorecard (units shown)

    metricvalueunit
    theoryT3_balanced_both_mass
    indirect_gap_err_meV8.8meV
    m_e_err0.0013mₑ
    m_h_err0.1855mₑ
    frontiergap<->m_h frontier real; m_h floor ~33% at gap<=9meV on sp(d) SK; S-3d(68p) diverges gap

    Hamiltonian compression — symbolic TB vs SIESTA

    384×parameter compression
    SIESTA H(k): 140 spinor orbitals (DZP) → dense 19600-entry matrix at every k.
    TB H(k): 26 orbitals · 51 symbolic parameters, analytic in k.
    Basis reduction 2.7× (70 → 26 orbitals).

    Fermi surface — TB H(k) vs SIESTA DFT (H, S)

    Green = our orthogonal TB; grey = the Fermi contour computed directly from SIESTA's own non-orthogonal Hamiltonian (read from the HSX). The overlap is the validation.

    H(k) — the winner Hamiltonian

    $$ H_{ij}(\mathbf{k}) \;=\; \varepsilon_i\,\delta_{ij}\;+\;\sum_{\mathbf{R}} w_{ij}^{\mathbf{R}}\,V_{\mathrm{key}}\,e^{\,i\,\mathbf{k}\cdot(\mathbf{R}\mathbf{A}+\boldsymbol{\tau}_j-\boldsymbol{\tau}_i)} $$

    εi = on-site orbital energy (eV) · Vkey = Slater–Koster two-centre integral (eV) · w = symmetry-fixed angular coefficient · τ = orbital positions · A = real-space cell.

    In2S2 — H(k) (T3_balanced_both_mass). SEMICONDUCTOR. Oblique III-VI, gap 1.948 eV.

    Orbital set S(s,p)+In(s,p,d), 26 bands. Real gap<->hole-mass frontier documented.

    H(k) — analytic form

    Phase convention (identical to tb_core and use_tb.py):

    H_ij(k) = ε_i · δ_ij                                  (on-site)
        + Σ_R  w_ij^R · V_key · e^{ i k·(R·A + τ_j − τ_i) }   (Slater-Koster hop)
  • ε_i — on-site orbital energies (E_<class>_<orbital>), shared by symmetry class.
  • V_key — fitted SK two-centre radial integrals, one per <integral>_<class-pair>_s<shell>; independent per shell.
  • w_ij^R — the exact SK angular coefficient for bond (i→j, R), stored in params.json (w); the model never hand-codes phases.
  • A — the real-space cell; τ — orbital positions (real, set the Bloch phases).

    • 26 orbitals → 26 bands · 51 parameters (13 on-site, 38 hopping).

    `Vsp_InS_s2`-4.3891212

    `Vss_InIn_s3`-5.886821
    `Vss_InIn_s4`-1.182182
    `Vss_InS_s1`-0.443634
    `Vss_InS_s2`+0.352532
    `Vss_SS_s5`-5.453612

    Notes & symmetry reasoning

    In₂S₂ — report


    ✅ RESOLUTION (shipped model = balanced both-mass fit)


    The exported tb_model/ is the T3 balanced fit (S(s,p)+In(s,p,d), 26 bands), found by a

    multi-objective multistart penalising gap + edges + both inverse masses simultaneously

    (the earlier T2/T3-curv runs penalised at most one mass). Independently re-measured from the

    saved artifact:


    PropertyDFTTBerrbarpass
    indirect gap1.94811.9393**8.8 meV**≤5~
    VBM−1.3289−1.317611.3 meV≤10~
    CBM0.61920.62172.5 meV≤10✅
    m_e*0.07760.0789**1.7 %**≤25✅
    m_h*−0.5568−0.3713**33 %**≤25✗

    Γ-verified 7.8×10⁻¹⁴ eV. This **more than halves the hole-mass error vs the old T2 winner

    (83 % → 33 %)** and brings m_e to ~2 %, at a small gap cost (0.3 → 8.8 meV, still excellent).


    The gap↔hole-mass frontier is real and was rigorously bounded (this supersedes the earlier

    "NEEDS HUMAN" verdict below, which is kept for the record):

  • On the sp(d) SK basis, m_h cannot go below ~33 % while keeping the gap ≤9 meV — every

    • solution with m_h ≤25 % has gap ≥25 meV (frontier scan, results/balanced_search.json).

  • S-3d polarisation does NOT break the frontier: a 36-band/68-param augmented basis diverges

    • the gap (>1.7 eV error in all solutions) — more freedom hurt, not helped.

  • Root cause: the heavy off-Γ zone-boundary hole (S-3p derived) and the In-s conduction edge are

    • coupled through shared two-centre integrals; the SK form cannot decouple them. Usable as-is for

    gap/edge/electron-transport; hole-transport carries the documented 33 % m_h caveat.


    Material

    In₂S₂ (In2S2-bb4d7aa7b2d9), 2D monolayer, Janus indium sulfide.

    Oblique III-VI semiconductor with pseudo-C2/m symmetry (P1 → C2/m at symprec 1e-2).


    Structure

  • 4 atoms/cell: In₂ + S₂
  • Lattice: a=3.931 Å, b=5.892 Å, c=33.096 Å, γ=109.55°
  • In-plane oblique (a≠b) — p_x, p_y, p_z independent on-sites
  • Species-shared parameters (C2/m pseudo-symmetry): S₀=S₁, In₂=In₃

    • DFT reference

  • DZP basis: 140 spinor → 70 spinless (Kramers-collapsed)
  • Indirect gap: 1.948 eV
  • VBM: −1.329 eV at k=449 (zone-boundary, off-Γ)
  • CBM: +0.619 eV at k=0 (Γ)
  • Electron effective mass: 0.078 m_e
  • Hole effective mass: −0.557 m_e

    • Theory ladder (exhausted)


    TheoryBasisBandsParamsGap errm_h*m_h* errm_e*m_e* errStatus
    T1 multistartS-p + In-sp142383 meV−0.05590%0.236203%too small
    T2 gap-focused (winner)S-p + In-spd24440.3 meV−0.09683%0.0718.5%m_h fails
    T2 (`score.json`)S-p + In-spd24441.3 meV−0.08385%0.05233%m_h fails
    T3 (`score_T3.json`)S-sp + In-spd26510.3 meV−0.5354.0%0.12966%m_e fails
    T3_curv (`score_T3_curv.json`)S-sp + In-spd265139.8 meV−1.972254%0.06911%gap + m_h fail

    No single theory satisfies all six properties within tolerance. T2 nails gap/edges/m_e but m_h fails. T3 nails gap/edges/m_h but m_e fails. T3_curv falls apart on gap and m_h.


    Best scorecard — T2 gap-focused combined (winner_score.json)


    PropertyDFTTB modelErrorTolerancePass
    Indirect gap1.9481 eV1.9484 eV0.3 meV≤5 meV✅
    Direct gap min2.2481 eV2.2669 eV19 meV——
    VBM−1.3289 eV−1.3191 eV9.8 meV≤10 meV✅
    CBM0.6192 eV0.6294 eV10.2 meV≤10 meV✅
    Electron mass m_e0.07760.07100.0066 (8.5%)≤25%✅
    Hole mass m_h−0.5568−0.09600.461 (83%)≤25%❌

    in-window RMSE [−4,5] eV: 179 meV

    in-window RMSE [−3,3] eV: 181 meV


    Γ-verification

    Export vs engine max deviation at all Γ-points: 4.6×10⁻¹⁴ eV ✅


    Files

    materials/In2S2-bb4d7aa7b2d9/
├── tb_model/
│   ├── formula.md          # H(k) analytic form
│   ├── params.json         # Numerical parameters
│   └── use_tb.py           # Python loader
├── results/
│   ├── score.json           # T2 final scorecard
│   ├── winner_score.json    # T2 gap-focused best scorecard
│   ├── score_T3.json        # T3 scorecard (m_h=4%, m_e=66%)
│   ├── score_T3_curv.json   # T3 curvature refit
│   ├── combined_best.json   # Best fit parameters (T2 gap-focused)
│   ├── ladder_T1.json       # T1 checkpoint
│   ├── ladder_T2.json       # T2 checkpoint
│   ├── ladder_T3.json       # T3 checkpoint
│   └── pysr_formulas.json   # PySR diagnostic
├── notebook.md              # Full scientific notebook
└── report.md                # This file


    VERDICT: NEEDS HUMAN — loop exhausted, m_h* unreachable


    Objective accept rule: FAILED


    The accept rule requires all properties within tolerance (gap ≤5 meV, edges ≤10 meV,

    masses ≤25%). Hole effective mass fails this in every theory — best achieved is 83%

    error (T2 gap-focused). The prior "ACCEPTED (basis-limited)" verdict was premature and

    is hereby retracted.


    Why m_h* is unreachable


    1. PySR diagnosed OPT-limited — VBM band individually spannable to ~3 meV on the

    T2 basis. The SK form *can* represent the band; the optimiser cannot find a single

    parameter set that satisfies gap + edges + m_e + m_h simultaneously.


    2. T3 proves the basis CAN capture m_h (−0.535 vs DFT −0.557, 4% error), but at

    the cost of m_e blowing up to 66% error. The heavy-hole and correct-m_e basins are

    disconnected in parameter space — S-3s couples valence and conduction bands.


    3. T3_curv proves m_e can be recovered (11% error) in the extended basis, but the

    gap blows to 40 meV and m_h overshoots to −1.97 (254%). No single parameter set

    satisfies gap + m_e + m_h jointly.


    4. The gap↔mass frontier is structural: every theory nails some properties and

    breaks others. The joint minimum for all six properties is unreachable on both the

    24-band and 26-band bases.


    What was tried (5+ distinct theories)


    TheoryDescriptionResult
    T1S-p + In-sp, 14-band, multistartToo small; m_e 203% off
    T2 gap-focusedS-p + In-spd, residual with gap/VBM/CBM penaltiesBest gap/edges/m_e; m_h 83% off
    T2 ladderS-p + In-spd, 30-restart multistartm_h 85% off, m_e 33% off
    T3S-p + In-spd + S-3s, 26-bandm_h fixed (4%), m_e broken (66%)
    T3_curvSame basis, curvature-weighted refit with mass penaltyGap broken (40 meV), m_h overshoots (254%)

    This is the GaS pattern


    Both In₂S₂ and GaS are oblique III-VI semiconductors with off-Γ VBM at the zone

    boundary. In both cases the joint gap+m_h* basin is unreachable in the Slater-Koster

    parameter space. The model is usable for gap/edge/electron-transport applications but

    not for hole-transport calculations.


    Human decision needed


  • Option A: Accept the T2 gap-focused model with documented m_h* caveat (83% error).

    • Gap, edges, and m_e are all within tolerance. Suitable for electron-transport

    simulations.

  • Option B: Attempt S-3d polarization orbitals (DFT DZP basis includes S-3d; the

    • additional angular character may decouple valence from conduction).

  • Option C: Accept and move on — the III-VI hole-mass limitation is a known pattern;

    • future work can revisit with a different fitting strategy (e.g. multi-objective GP).



    Limitations


    1. Hole effective mass (83% error): The off-Γ VBM at the zone boundary has complex

    curvature. T3 proves the 26-band basis *can* capture it (4% error), but joint

    optimization across all properties collapses to the lighter-hole basin. This is a

    landscape topology problem, not a basis-completeness problem.


    2. Whole-band RMSE (~2.4 eV): The In-4d orbitals produce large errors in bands far

    from the gap (>4 eV). Acceptable for a semiconductor model targeting near-gap

    transport properties.


    3. No SOC: Kramers pairs are collapsed. λL·S coupling is left for future

    spin-dependent extensions.


    API

    import sys; sys.path.insert(0, "materials/In2S2-bb4d7aa7b2d9/tb_model")
import use_tb
H, info = use_tb.load()
bands = use_tb.bands(H, k_points)  # k_points in fractional coords

    NiTe₂ semimetal

    📐 P-6̄m2 (#187) 🧩 basis: Nia_dx2-y2, Nia_dxy, Nia_dyz, Nia_dz2, Nia_dzx, Nia_px, Nia_py, Nia_pz, Nia_s, Tea_px, Tea_py, Tea_pz, Tea_s ⚙️ 49 params 🗜 324× vs SIESTA

    Band structure — DFT (grey) vs TB H(k) (green)

    Computed scorecard (units shown)

    metricvalueunit
    theoryT4_balanced_fermi
    near_ef_wrmse_meV166.3meV
    tight_ef_meV54meV
    ef_crossings_dft8
    ef_crossings_tb8
    fermi_velocity_dft5.136eV·Å
    fermi_velocity_tb6.112eV·Å

    Hamiltonian compression — symbolic TB vs SIESTA

    324×parameter compression
    SIESTA H(k): 126 spinor orbitals (DZP) → dense 15876-entry matrix at every k.
    TB H(k): 17 orbitals · 49 symbolic parameters, analytic in k.
    Basis reduction 3.7× (63 → 17 orbitals).

    Fermi surface — TB H(k) vs SIESTA DFT (H, S)

    Green = our orthogonal TB; grey = the Fermi contour computed directly from SIESTA's own non-orthogonal Hamiltonian (read from the HSX). The overlap is the validation.

    Spin–orbit coupling & spin texture (local λL·S)

    On-site SOC added to the orthogonal model; spin texture = ⟨ψ|S|ψ⟩ on the Fermi surface. Only non-trivial without inversion symmetry.

    Fermi contour + spin texture (S_xy arrows, S_z colour)

    PySR symbolic study — per-band closed forms vs numeric

    PySR over all targeted bands: each band E_n(k) as a closed form in the orthogonal-basis Bloch sums (S=I, scored through the diagonalizer). Interpretable + accurate per band, but N independent fits — not one H. The unified radial-law H does not beat the numeric fit on a hard orthogonal material (amplitudes irreducible). See notes/pysr_runs.md.

    H(k) — the winner Hamiltonian

    $$ H_{ij}(\mathbf{k}) \;=\; \varepsilon_i\,\delta_{ij}\;+\;\sum_{\mathbf{R}} w_{ij}^{\mathbf{R}}\,V_{\mathrm{key}}\,e^{\,i\,\mathbf{k}\cdot(\mathbf{R}\mathbf{A}+\boldsymbol{\tau}_j-\boldsymbol{\tau}_i)} $$

    εi = on-site orbital energy (eV) · Vkey = Slater–Koster two-centre integral (eV) · w = symmetry-fixed angular coefficient · τ = orbital positions · A = real-space cell.

    NiTe2 (2D, 1H P-6m2 D3h) — H(k) (T4, class-shared Ni/Te, balanced Fermi fit). METAL.


    P-6m2 (#187, D3h, no inversion); hexagonal px=py; trigonal-prismatic 1H (NOT 1T). Entangled Ni-3d/Te-5p, NO gap (8 E_F crossings). Spin-averaged; SOC (68 meV) = lambda L.S extension. Scorecard: results/winner_score.json.

    H(k) — analytic form

    Phase convention (identical to tb_core and use_tb.py):

    H_ij(k) = ε_i · δ_ij                                  (on-site)
        + Σ_R  w_ij^R · V_key · e^{ i k·(R·A + τ_j − τ_i) }   (Slater-Koster hop)
  • ε_i — on-site orbital energies (E_<class>_<orbital>), shared by symmetry class.
  • V_key — fitted SK two-centre radial integrals, one per <integral>_<class-pair>_s<shell>; independent per shell.
  • w_ij^R — the exact SK angular coefficient for bond (i→j, R), stored in params.json (w); the model never hand-codes phases.
  • A — the real-space cell; τ — orbital positions (real, set the Bloch phases).

    • 17 orbitals → 17 bands · 49 parameters (13 on-site, 36 hopping).
